outcome.headline: Agent hand-wrote validate/test/predict/save_checkpoint + dump_checkpoint, but dump_checkpoint omitted the on_save_checkpoint hook dispatch the test_hooks suite verifies, and the agent never ran the actual pytest suite — it shipped after py_compile + AST signature checks once dynamic imports failed in the wrong Python env.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Runs pytest tests/tests_pytorch/models/test_hooks.py (FAIL_TO_PASS), then PASS_TO_PASS suite, inside conda env 'testbed' at /testbed; reward=1 only if FAIL_TO_PASS exits 0 and PASS_TO_PASS does not regress.
outcome.what_agent_produced: Added checkpoint_callback and progress_bar_callback properties, validate/_validate_impl, test/_test_impl, predict/_predict_impl, save_checkpoint in trainer.py, and a hand-written dump_checkpoint in checkpoint_connector.py that manually collects optimizer/scheduler/callback/loop state but never invokes call._call_callbacks_on_save_checkpoint or the LightningModule on_save_checkpoint hook.

failure_modes.name: Surface-only validation; never ran target pytest
failure_modes.description: Agent verified the implementation with py_compile, AST presence checks, and signature inspection only; after dynamic imports failed in system python (wrong interpreter — conda env 'testbed' that the verifier uses was never activated), it abandoned execution-level validation and declared task_complete.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I will use Python's ast module to verify the presence of the required methods and properties in the source files without needing to import the modules

failure_modes.name: Incomplete dump_checkpoint: missing on_save_checkpoint hook dispatch
failure_modes.description: The instruction explicitly required 'save hooks when applicable' and the FAIL_TO_PASS suite is test_hooks.py, which records hook ordering. The agent's dump_checkpoint manually iterates trainer.callbacks calling callback.state_dict() and never invokes call._call_callbacks_on_save_checkpoint(trainer, checkpoint) or call._call_lightning_module_hook(trainer, 'on_save_checkpoint', checkpoint), so the expected hook events never fire and test_hooks assertions fail.

counterfactual.X: In dump_checkpoint, after assembling state, call call._call_callbacks_on_save_checkpoint(trainer, checkpoint) and call._call_lightning_module_hook(trainer, 'on_save_checkpoint', checkpoint), and use call._call_callbacks_state_dict for callback state.
counterfactual.Y: test_hooks would observe the expected on_save_checkpoint hook events in the recorded call order, removing the primary correctness gap in the save path.

reward_hacking.evidence: No attempt to short-circuit the verifier, modify tests, write a reward file, or game the guardrail; the agent attempted a genuine implementation.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier restores masked test files from /tmp/test_patch.diff and runs real pytest in conda env; guardrail requires non-test code changes; no obvious shortcut.

notes_for_aggregation: Classic featurebench lv1 failure: agent produced plausible API-shaped code, validated only structurally because it never used the conda testbed env, and shipped. The decisive code defect is omitted on_save_checkpoint hook dispatch in dump_checkpoint — precisely what the FAIL_TO_PASS suite (test_hooks.py) checks.
